description: eREVEALER (enhanced Repeated Evaluation of VariablEs conditionAL Entropy and Redundancy) is an analysis method specifically suited to find groups of genomic alterations that match in a complementary way, a predefined functional activation, dependency of drug response target profile. The method starts by considering, if available, already known genomic alterations (seed) that are the known causes or are known or assumed associated with the target. REVEALER starts by finding the genomic alteration that best matches the target profile conditional to the known seed profile using the conditional mutual information. The newly discovered alteration is then merged with the seed to form a new summary feature, and then the process repeats itself finding additional complementary alterations that explain more and more of the target profile.
author: Pablo Tamayo;Broad Institute
plan:
## eREVEALER GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
eREVEALER is a GenePattern module that eREVEALER (enhanced Repeated Evaluation of VariablEs conditionAL Entropy and Redundancy) is an analysis method specifically suited to find groups of genomic alterations that match in a complementary way, a predefined functional activation, dependency of drug response target profile. The method starts by considering, if available, already known genomic alterations (seed) that are the known causes or are known or assumed associated with the target. REVEALER starts by finding the genomic alteration that best matches the target profile conditional to the known seed profile using the conditional mutual information. The newly discovered alteration is then merged with the seed to form a new summary feature, and then the process repeats itself finding additional complementary alterations that explain more and more of the target profile.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 32 parameters:
- Required: none
- Optional: target_file, feature_files, target_name, direction, gene_locus, prefix, seed_files, seed_name, k_size, bandwidth_multiplication, bandwidth_adjustment, alpha, neighborhood, gene_set, separator, subset, feature_remove, feature_set, low_threshold, high_threshold, normalize, figure_format, if_intermediate, if_pvalue, if_cluster, if_bootstrap, if_collapse, num_top, max_iteration, verbose, tissue_file, gmt_file

### 3. Command Line
```
REVEALER run --target_file <target_file> --feature_files <feature_files> <seed_files> --prefix <prefix>  <seed_name> --target_name <target_name> --k_size <k_size> --bandwidth_multiplication <bandwidth_multiplication> --bandwidth_adjustment <bandwidth_adjustment> --direction <direction> --num_top <num_top> --low_threshold <low_threshold> --high_threshold <high_threshold> --if_collapse <if_collapse> --figure_format <figure_format> --normalize <normalize> --gene_locus <gene_locus> --verbose <verbose> --max_iteration <max_iteration> <subset> --if_pvalue <if_pvalue> --if_bootstrap <if_bootstrap> --if_cluster <if_cluster> --if_intermediate <if_intermediate> --separator <separator> <gene_set> <gmt_file> --alpha <alpha>  <tissue_file> --neighborhood <neighborhood> <feature_remove> <feature_set>\n
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: yoshihiko1218/revealer\:v0.0.7

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: target_file
parameters.description: Target profile dataset

parameters.name: feature_files
parameters.description: Features Dataset (e.g. https\://datasets.genepattern.org/?prefix\=data/module_support_files/REVEALER/CCLE_Mut_All.gct)

parameters.name: target_name
parameters.description: Target profile name (e.g. BETA-CATENIN-REPORTER)

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Direction of match (e.g. positive)

parameters.name: gene_locus
parameters.description: Table with chromosomal location for each gene symbol (e.g. https\://personal.broadinstitute.org/tamayo/REVEALER/datasets/hgnc_complete_set.Feb_20_2014.v2.txt)

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Prefix of output files with results (e.g. BCAT_vs_MUT_CNA)

parameters.name: seed_files
parameters.description: Features Dataset that contain seeds. If same file with feature file, then keep this blank.

parameters.name: seed_name
parameters.description: seed(s) names (e.g. CTNNB1.MC_MUT)

parameters.name: k_size
parameters.description: Size of kernel indicating k variance far from middle. Has to be int. Default is 5

parameters.name: bandwidth_multiplication
parameters.description: Value of bandwidth multiplier. Has to be float or int. Default is 0.65.

parameters.name: bandwidth_adjustment
parameters.description: Value of bandwidth adjustment. Has to be float or int. Default is -0.95

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Power to raise value. Default is 1.

parameters.name: neighborhood
parameters.description: This indicates number of neighborhood for kernel. Higher number will lead to higher accuracy but lower speed.

parameters.name: gene_set
parameters.description: Gene Set that is extracted. File format should be txt file with single column indicating genes.

parameters.name: separator
parameters.description: Separator between gene name and later part.

parameters.name: subset
parameters.description: Subset of samples to be chosen. Has to be file with one column with subset of sample names in target file. Default is use all columns. File format should be txt file with single column indicating samples.

parameters.name: feature_remove
parameters.description: features to remove manually. This one is specifically for some very universal features that interrupt calculation. File format should be txt file with single column indicating features.

parameters.name: feature_set
parameters.description: features to use specifically. This one is used if interested in interaction between some specific features. File format should be txt file with single column indicating features.

parameters.name: low_threshold
parameters.description: Filter out features with less than low_threshold 1's (e.g. 3)

parameters.name: high_threshold
parameters.description: Filter out features with more than high_threshold 1's (e.g. 50)

parameters.name: normalize
parameters.description: Way to normalize the target. Can be standard or zerobase. Default is zerobase.

parameters.name: figure_format
parameters.description: Format for figure. Can be any figure format available in matplotlib. Default is pdf.

parameters.name: if_intermediate
parameters.description: Indicate if intermediate reports are generated. Has to be True or False. Default is True.

parameters.name: if_pvalue
parameters.description: Indicate if pvalues are calculated and plotted on figure. Has to be True or False. Default is False.

parameters.name: if_cluster
parameters.description: Indicate if features are clustered using NMF for intermediate report. Has to be True or False. Default is False.

parameters.name: if_bootstrap
parameters.description: Indicate if variance are calculated by bootstrap and plotted on figure. Has to be True or False. Default is False.

parameters.name: if_collapse
parameters.description: Indicate if features are collapsed for intermediate report. Has to be True or False. Default is False.

parameters.name: num_top
parameters.description: Number of top hits to show in heatmap for every iteration (e.g. 30)

parameters.name: max_iteration
parameters.description: Maximum number of iterations (e.g. 2, if set to -1, it will stop automatically when IC stop increasing)

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Verbose level for report. Setting 0 to get no report. Default is 1.

parameters.name: tissue_file
parameters.description: File of tissue information. This will create a output that have tissue distribution information of samples after sorting. File format should be gct file indicating type of tissue of sample.

parameters.name: gmt_file
parameters.description: gmt file for allele information. Usually got from preprocessing step.
